findings.claim: Only ptr_app, ptr_ui, ptr_ui_controls, and ptr_server accept an unquoted ggplot expression — they each call ptr_capture_formula(rlang::enexpr(formula), envir); ptr_init_state (and thus ptr_extract*/ptr_gg_extra), ptr_id_table, ptr_app_bslib, and ptr_app_grid are string-only.
findings.detail: ptr_init_state does tree <- ptr_translate(formula, expr_check=expr_check) with no enexpr capture; ptr_id_table asserts is.character(formula). So roxygen examples may lead with the expression form ONLY for the four boundary fns; the rest must stay string examples.
findings.verification: Confirmed ptr_capture_formula(rlang::enexpr(formula), envir) at app.R:141,162 (both in ptr_app body, def@134), 882 (ptr_ui@873), 989 (ptr_ui_controls@981), 1130 (ptr_server@1128) — the four boundary fns. server.R:132 is `tree <- ptr_translate(formula, ...)` (string, no capture); ptr_id_table asserts is.character at input-spec.R:241. NOTE: line drift since stamping — ptr_ui_controls capture 983->989, ptr_server 1124->1130 (my edits added lines); claim unchanged.

findings.claim: ptr_capture_formula resolves a bare symbol bound to a string or call by eval_bare in envir then a fallback walk over sys.frames(), so `f <- rlang::expr(ggplot(...)); ptr_ui(f, "id")` works WITHOUT `!!` (and `!!f` also works because enexpr supports injection).
findings.detail: It accepts only character/call/symbol values (guard against poisoning upstream frames), unwraps a top-level { ... } block (keeps last expr), and aborts if a named variable resolves to neither string nor expression. Canonical docs lead with `!!` but bare-symbol resolution is equivalent for a plain variable.
findings.verification: ptr_capture_formula def@196; is_symbol branch@210; tryCatch(eval_bare(formula_captured, envir))@221; fallback `for (fr in rev(sys.frames()))`@228; call/symbol fall-through @289. Symbol-to-value resolution path confirmed present as claimed.

findings.claim: test-llm.R asserts "every ptr_*() call in the LLM corpus (inst/llm/) names an exported function", so unexporting any function that the corpus documents as a call breaks the suite, and the pre-commit hook (which runs dev/build-llm.R) BLOCKS the commit on that drift.
findings.detail: This is why unexporting ptr_app_bslib/ptr_app_grid rippled far beyond roxygen: the corpus taught them as public L1 entry points across 8 files (overview, primer, level1_ptr_app incl. a whole "Multiple plots" grid section, formula_syntax, level1_ptr_options, level2_ui_text, level3_layout). Pattern: an unexport is an API-surface change that must be propagated to the corpus + generator, not just NAMESPACE.
findings.verification: test_that("every ptr_*() call in the corpus names an exported function") at test-llm.R:107 (assert ~117), reading llm_corpus_files()@19 via ptr_calls_in()@26 vs getNamespaceExports. pre-commit hook (lines 86-93) runs BOTH `Rscript dev/build-llm.R lint` (line 88) and `verify` (line 92) when inst/llm/ or dev/build-llm.R is staged, failing the commit on either. Minor: test_that is line 107 not 117; hook runs lint AND verify (stamp said just lint).

findings.claim: dev/build-llm.R only regenerates two @ptr-gen managed-block types — "token-table" and "topic-index" (both in primer.md) — from hardcoded TOKEN_ROWS/TOPIC_DESC lists; the function-signature bullets in primer.md are plain hand-authored prose (there is NO "level-table" managed block).
findings.detail: Commands: lint (HARD-fails on any non-export ptr_*() call, exit 1), generate (rewrite managed blocks in place), verify (sync gate, exit 1 on drift). Because TOPIC_DESC is hardcoded, removing a function reference requires editing BOTH the .md prose AND the TOPIC_DESC string in build-llm.R, else generate reintroduces the stale ref. lint is the authoritative corpus gate, not just verify.
findings.verification: render_block switch (lines 291-292) handles only "token-table" and "topic-index"; no "level-table" string exists in the file. TOKEN_ROWS@234, TOPIC_DESC@258 are hardcoded literals. lint@132, verify@331 present. Confirmed fixing TOPIC_DESC was required this session (else `generate` re-added ptr_app_grid() to primer's topic-index).

findings.claim: ptr_app_bslib() and ptr_app_grid() were unexported (changed @export to @keywords internal), which keeps their man/*.Rd generated so existing [ptr_app_grid()]/[ptr_app_bslib()] cross-reference links still resolve, while dropping them from NAMESPACE.
findings.detail: Tests call them directly (e.g. ptr_app_grid_components, test-app-bslib.R) and still pass because devtools::test() loads internals. Full suite green at a675550: FAIL 0 / WARN 4 / SKIP 0 / PASS 3523. The two functions remain reachable via ggpaintr:::.
findings.verification: NAMESPACE has no export of either (grep NONE); @keywords internal present at paintr-app-bslib.R:54 (and on ptr_app_grid in paintr-app.R); man/ptr_app_bslib.Rd and man/ptr_app_grid.Rd both still generated. Full-suite run on committed a675550 = FAIL 0/WARN 4/SKIP 0/PASS 3523 (721s, serial, browser on).

findings.claim: A bare ggplot(...) call in a non-interactive @examples block runs under R CMD check because package examples execute with ggpaintr (and its attached ggplot2) loaded, so expression-form examples need no library(ggplot2) and no interactive() wrapper to pass run_examples.
findings.detail: Confirmed by the pre-existing unwrapped ptr_ui example at R/paintr-app.R:866 and by run_examples passing after converting ptr_ui_controls to the expression form. Shiny-app-launching examples still need if (interactive()).
findings.verification: R/paintr-app.R:866 is the unwrapped `ui <- ptr_ui(ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = ppVar, y = ppVar)) + geom_point(), "plot1")` example (no interactive(), no library(ggplot2)). devtools::run_examples(run_dontrun=FALSE) printed RUN_EXAMPLES_OK this session after the expression-form conversions.
